refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under controlled duty cycle conditions with continuous telemetry capture of cycle start/end timestamps, segmented by phase (load, travel, dump). Statistical analysis of p95 percentile against CT_target baseline. Comparison of autonomous vs. tele-operated vs. manual modes if applicable.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump motions with deterministic cycle phase timing.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall maintain operator command responsiveness with latency ≤ latency_budget to avoid cycle time degradation.
FR-2.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time penalty.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration) to enable KPI tracking.

candidate_dps: DP-2.A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation response time and motion profiles; implement tele-operation with low-latency network link; enforce safety interlocks with minimal timing overhead.
DP-2.B: Implement cycle time monitoring and adaptive duty cycle adjustment based on real-time telemetry feedback to maintain p95 ≤ CT_target.

DP-2.C: Decompose cycle time into phase-level SLAs (load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget) and allocate to vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems; implement closed-loop cycle time control.

DP-2.D: Establish baseline cycle time under nominal conditions; implement performance degradation model to predict p95 under variable site conditions (terrain, load mass, operator skill); use predictive telemetry to alert if p95 trajectory exceeds CT_target.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2.C: Decompose cycle time into phase-level SLAs (load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget) and allocate to vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems; implement closed-loop cycle time control with telemetry-based monitoring and adaptive constraint relaxation within safety envelope.

potential_sources: Safety constraints (FR-2.3) may couple with vehicle motion timing (FR-2.1) if safety interlocks introduce unavoidable latency; mitigation: design safety logic to operate in parallel with motion control, not serially.
Teleoperation latency (FR-2.2) couples with vehicle responsiveness (FR-2.1); mitigation: allocate latency budget across network link and vehicle control loop.
Telemetry reporting (FR-2.4) may introduce computational overhead on vehicle controller; mitigation: use asynchronous telemetry buffering to avoid blocking motion control.
coupling_check.notes: DP-2.C is selected because it explicitly allocates cycle time budgets to subsystems, enabling independent optimization and clear ownership. Coupling is manageable via interface contracts (latency budgets, safety overhead caps) and parallel execution architecture.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: CT_target is achievable under nominal duty cycle with current vehicle actuation technology (hydraulic/electric response time ≥ 100 ms per phase).
ASSUMPTION: Network link latency is ≤ 50 ms round-trip for tele-operation to remain transparent to cycle time.
ASSUMPTION: Safety interlocks can be implemented with ≤ 20 ms overhead per cycle phase.
RISK: If site conditions (terrain, load variability) cause p95 to exceed CT_target, adaptive constraint relaxation may conflict with safety margins; requires safety case review.
RISK: Closed-loop cycle time control may oscillate if feedback gains are poorly tuned; requires control system validation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2.C to explicitly define the control law for adaptive constraint relaxation (e.g., which safety constraints are relaxable, under what conditions, with what residual risk acceptance). Add requirement for cycle time control stability analysis and tuning procedure.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level cycle time orchestration, phase-level SLA allocation, closed-loop monitoring, and adaptive control. Owns the cycle time KPI and coordinates across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Cycle time phase timestamps (load_start, load_end, travel_start, travel_end, dump_start, dump_end), total cycle duration, p95 percentile, and performance vs. CT_target status. Published at 1 Hz or on-demand.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, operator mode: autonomous/tele-op/manual) and CT_target baseline. Updated per job or shift.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety constraint status and overhead budget utilization from Safety Services. Signals if safety overhead is approaching or exceeding budget, triggering adaptive control decisions.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Latency measurement and quality-of-service (QoS) feedback from Teleoperation subsystem. Used to adjust tele-operation responsiveness budget and predict cycle time impact.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level orchestrator and owns cycle time KPI management, phase-level SLA allocation, and closed-loop control logic. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry reporting and interface coordination.

Vehicle owns motion actuation and phase timing execution but does not own cycle time orchestration or SLA allocation. Cycle time control logic must reside at NGHE Solution level to coordinate across subsystems.
Teleoperation owns operator command latency but not cycle time SLA allocation or closed-loop control. Cycle time impact of tele-operation is managed at NGHE Solution level.
Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement but not cycle time optimization. Safety overhead budget is managed at NGHE Solution level via SafetyAlert interface.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI management (DP-2.C) is a system-level concern that requires orchestration across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. NGHE Solution is the appropriate boundary because it owns the phase-level SLA allocation logic, closed-loop control, and telemetry-based monitoring. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are subordinate modules that execute their allocated budgets and report status via interfaces.
containment_risks: RISK: If cycle time control logic is distributed across Vehicle and NGHE Solution, SLA allocation and feedback loops may become inconsistent. Mitigation: centralize all cycle time orchestration in NGHE Solution; Vehicle executes motion within allocated time budgets.

RISK: Telemetry reporting overhead may impact Vehicle controller performance if not properly decoupled. Mitigation: implement asynchronous telemetry buffering in Vehicle; NGHE Solution polls telemetry at lower frequency (1 Hz) than motion control (100 Hz).

RISK: Adaptive constraint relaxation logic may require real-time safety case updates, creating coupling between NGHE Solution and Safety Services. Mitigation: pre-define relaxation scenarios and residual risk acceptance in safety case; NGHE Solution operates within pre-approved envelope.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the interface contract between NGHE Solution and Vehicle: (1) NGHE Solution allocates phase time budgets to Vehicle; (2) Vehicle reports actual phase completion times and constraint violations; (3) NGHE Solution adjusts future budgets based on feedback. Add requirement for telemetry buffering architecture to decouple reporting from motion control.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-2.C (phase-level SLA allocation with closed-loop control) directly addresses FR-2 (achieve p95 ≤ CT_target). The DP is specific, measurable, and allocates responsibility to subsystems. Coupling is manageable via interface contracts. Assumptions and risks are documented and acceptable for proceeding to IM assignment.

diffusion_risks: Cycle time control logic is centralized in NGHE Solution, which is appropriate for system-level orchestration. Risk of diffusion is low if Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services strictly adhere to allocated budgets and report status via interfaces.
Telemetry reporting must be asynchronously decoupled from motion control to avoid diffusion of timing concerns into Vehicle controller. Recommend explicit telemetry buffering module or pattern.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct boundary for cycle time KPI management because it owns the orchestration logic and SLA allocation. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are subordinate modules that execute within allocated budgets. Interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and enable independent optimization.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI can be verified via instrumented field trials with continuous telemetry capture. Phase timestamps are measurable and p95 percentile is computable. Verification is feasible if telemetry infrastructure is in place.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Phase-level SLA allocation is clear: load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget are allocated to Vehicle; latency_budget is allocated to Teleoperation; safety_overhead_budget is allocated to Safety Services. NGHE Solution owns orchestration and monitoring. Allocation is traceable to sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4).

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-2.C to define the adaptive constraint relaxation control law and pre-approved scenarios. Add safety case review gate before implementation.
Define telemetry buffering architecture to decouple reporting from motion control. Specify buffer size, polling frequency, and overflow handling.
Add requirement for cycle time control stability analysis and tuning procedure. Specify acceptable overshoot, settling time, and robustness margins.
Define interface contracts (latency budgets, safety overhead caps, phase time budgets) as formal specifications with acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: FR-2 (cycle time KPI) is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) to address vehicle motion timing, teleoperation latency, safety overhead, and telemetry monitoring. DP-2.C is sound and well-allocated to NGHE Solution, but sub-FRs must be refined and decomposed further to enable detailed design and implementation. Proceeding to next level of functional decomposition is necessary.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump motions with deterministic cycle phase timing within allocated time budgets (load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall maintain operator command responsiveness with latency ≤ latency_budget to avoid cycle time degradation.
FR-2.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time penalty.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration, p95 percentile) to enable KPI tracking and adaptive control.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable under nominal duty cycle with current vehicle actuation technology.
Network link latency is ≤ 50 ms round-trip for tele-operation.
Safety interlocks can be implemented with ≤ 20 ms overhead per cycle phase.
Adaptive constraint relaxation scenarios are pre-defined and safety-approved.
Telemetry reporting can be asynchronously decoupled from motion control without loss of fidelity.
open_risks: Site conditions (terrain, load variability) may cause p95 to exceed CT_target, requiring adaptive constraint relaxation that may conflict with safety margins.
Closed-loop cycle time control may oscillate if feedback gains are poorly tuned.
Telemetry reporting overhead may impact Vehicle controller performance if not properly decoupled.
Adaptive constraint relaxation logic may require real-time safety case updates, creating coupling between NGHE Solution and Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Baseline cycle time measurements under nominal duty cycle conditions.
Network link latency characterization (round-trip latency distribution, jitter, packet loss).
Safety overhead budget quantification (timing impact of safety interlocks per phase).
Vehicle actuation response time characterization (load, travel, dump phase timing under various load masses and terrain types).
Telemetry infrastructure design and performance validation (buffer size, polling frequency, overhead).
Cycle time control stability analysis and tuning procedure.
Safety case review and approval for adaptive constraint relaxation scenarios.
